rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO ARCHITECTURAL HERITAGE , A VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 4, 2012, Line 4, REAL PARTY IN INTEREST PACIFIC HEIGHTS FRANKLIN PARTNERS LLC, PACIFIC HEIGHTS FRANKLIN PARTNERS II LLC'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the real parties in interest after deleting the references to dismissing the action. The appropriate procedure for civil lawsuits after a demurrer is sustained without leave to amend is for judgment to be entered in favor of the demurring parties. If there is a different procedure for a writ, real parties in interest should bring authority to the hearing supporting the different procedure. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. TAD V NGUYEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 4, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU'S Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. Motion granted in part; denied in part. Plaintiffs' motion for an assignment order under CCP §708.510(a) and a "turnover" order under CCP §699.040(a) are granted. Plaintiffs' motion for a restraining order under CCP §708.520(a) is denied. Plaintiffs' motion under CCP §708.110(a) is taken off calendar and should be reset for Department 514 per the procedures set forth in Local Rule 8.9. Plaintiffs shall present by 9am on Thursday 10/4/12 an order for signature to the Court that corresponds with this tentative. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EDWIN LOW et al VS. SUSANNE LOW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 4, 2012, Line 7, CROSS DEFENDANTS EDWIN LOW, CONNIE LOW YAMAMOTO, JENNIFER HOVLAND'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the ninth cause of action for nuisance. Susanne has not adequately pled nuisance based on her allegations of being locked out. Phillips v. City of Pasadena (1945) 27 Cal.2d 104 is distinguishable as it involved access to a road. Nor is there a reasonable possibility that Susanne can cure this deficiency by an amendment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN A. BURMEISTER, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE VS. 4055 SONOMA PROPERTIES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Fix And Award Attorneys' Fes And For An Award Of Costs Of Defts 4055 Sonoma Properties Llc And Diana Saca [Per Ccp 1021, 1032 And 1033.5]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 4, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANT 4055 SONOMA PROPERTIES, LLC, DIANA SACA'S Motion To Fix And Award Attorneys' Fes And For An Award Of Costs Of Defendants 4055 Sonoma Properties LLC And Diana Saca [Per CCP 1021, 1032 And 1033.5] Denied. Paragraph 5A of the Exclusive Easement Agreement applies to contract claims only. Plaintiff's claims sounded in tort. The references in the complaint and amended complaint to the Exclusive Easement Agreement and the improper prayer to have it declared void do not change the nature of the causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA POLIZZI VS. JEFFREY A FELDMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 4, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT JEFFREY FELDMAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Counsel for Defendant has requested oral argument on the tentative ruling issued by the Court on 9/7/12 which stated: Motion granted in part; denied in part. Plaintiff has agreed to provide supplemental responses to the outstanding RFPD's. Those supplemental responses shall be served no later than September 21, 2012. The Court notes that the only requirement of CCP §2031 is a statement similar to "All responsive, non-privilege documents" have been produced. Plaintiffs are also directed to ascertain whether they can improve the visual clarity of the documents Defendant contends are illegible. As for the interrogatories at issue: the motion is granted as to Form Interrogatory No 17.1 - RFA Nos. 8, 11, 12, 34, 35, 36, 37; Interrogatory Nos. 7, 10, 13. The motion is denied as to Form Interrogatory No. 17.1, - RFA Nos. 4, 10, 16, 24, 29, 30, 31, 32, 33, 42, 43; Interrogatory Nos. 12, 28, 34. All requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JANNA CRANDALL VS. CHUBB CUSTOM INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 4, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT LA GALLERIA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted without leave to amend as to the punitive damages allegations and granted with leave to amend as to the prayer for attorney's fees. Plaintiff still fails to allege facts that rise to the level of malice, fraud, or oppression and it does not appear that granting further leave to amend would cure this deficiency. Plaintiff is granted leave to allege, if she can do so in good faith, facts sufficient to support her request for attorney's fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM PFLUGH et al VS. 2-4-6-8-10-12 STEINER STREET et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 4, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANTS 2-4-6-8-10-12 STEINER STREET, DEBI GUNDERS, JOHN MCLAY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is denied. Plaintiffs have pled sufficient facts to maintain a claim for punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM PFLUGH et al VS. 2-4-6-8-10-12 STEINER STREET et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 4, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANTS 2-4-6-8-10-12 STEINER STREET, DEBI GUNDERS, JOHN MCLAY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action and overruled as to all other causes of action. Plaintiffs have not alleged any facts that implicate the type of conduct the UCL was intended to address. It does not appear that an amendment could cure the defect. All other causes of action are adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WENDY OAKES VS. CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, October 4, 2012, Line 18, DEFENDANT CELTIC MOVING & STORAGE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Continued on the court's own motion to October 18, 2012. Plaintiff shall serve her opposition on Defendant by email or personal delivery no later than 4pm October 4. Any reply shall be filed and served in accordance with Code of Civil Procedure § 1005(b) as to the new hearing date. Courtesy copies are to be provided to Department 302 on the same date as filing. =(302/HEK)
